agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being scoped where customers would see their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator, while Operations demands zero location sharing due to driver retention problems. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that cannot be resolved at the engineering level.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Courier Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"The driver or delivery person who physically transports a package from origin to destination."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The courier's GPS coordinates updated at frequent intervals during an active delivery."},{"term":"Delivery window","definition":"The time period during which a courier is actively en route to the customer's delivery address."},{"term":"Location granularity","definition":"The precision at which a courier's position is exposed — ranging from exact GPS coordinates to zone-level approximations (e.g., '2 stops away', 'nearby')."},{"term":"Courier privacy","definition":"The operational and legal principle that a courier's precise movements should not be unnecessarily exposed to external parties."},{"term":"Customer visibility","definition":"The degree to which a customer can observe the state and progress of their delivery in real time."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (e.g., traffic, break) and exact GPS is shared","expected_behavior":"Customer may perceive delivery as abandoned or courier as idle, generating support tickets and negative experience — while courier is performing legitimate work"},{"scenario":"Courier delivers to a sensitive or dangerous area","expected_behavior":"Sharing exact GPS could create safety risks for the courier beyond just surveillance feelings"},{"scenario":"Courier is multi-dropping (delivering multiple orders on one route)","expected_behavior":"Exact GPS reveals the courier is not heading directly to this customer, causing frustration despite being operationally efficient"},{"scenario":"Customer uses exact GPS to intercept courier en route","expected_behavior":"Creates safety and operational risks — courier confronted away from delivery point"},{"scenario":"Courier's personal stop (bathroom, fuel) is visible to customer","expected_behavior":"Privacy violation and source of complaints from both couriers and customers"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Unresolved either way: showing exact GPS risks accelerating driver churn (recruiting and training replacement drivers is typically 3-5x more expensive than retention). Not showing location risks losing the competitive differentiator that Customer Success believes drives acquisition and retention of customers. Both revenue streams are at stake.","operational":"Driver retention is an existential operational concern — a courier network with high turnover degrades delivery quality, increases costs, and reduces coverage. However, customer transparency expectations are rising industry-wide and failing to meet them creates support load and churn.","user":"Two distinct user groups in direct conflict. Customers want maximum visibility. Couriers want minimum surveillance. Optimizing for one actively harms the other. Any solution must balance both sides of this two-sided marketplace.","risk":"HIGH. Shipping either requirement as stated will cause measurable damage to the other stakeholder group. Additionally, exact GPS sharing may implicate GDPR, CCPA, or local labor privacy regulations — courier location data is personal data in many jurisdictions, and sharing it with third parties (customers) without proper legal basis is a compliance risk.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Desire real-time delivery visibility to reduce anxiety and plan their availability. Losing this feature or never getting it may reduce satisfaction and competitive positioning."},{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"Report feeling surveilled when exact location is shared. This is causing measurable attrition. Driver wellbeing and retention is foundational to the business."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Believes transparency is a key market differentiator. Needs a visibility feature to sell against competitors."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Owns driver retention metrics. Has data showing drivers are leaving because of location surveillance."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Not yet consulted but must be — sharing courier GPS with customers has privacy regulation implications in most jurisdictions."},{"group":"Executive leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict. This is a strategic business decision about marketplace balance, not an engineering or product decision."}]},"confidence":{"level":35,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct, unresolvable contradiction between two non-negotiable requirements from peer-level stakeholder teams. Neither requirement can be implemented without violating the other. Additionally, Legal/Compliance has not weighed in, which could invalidate either position. No executive arbitration has occurred. Confidence is very low because there is no coherent spec to build against.","high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction is real and cannot be papered over with clever engineering","Both stakeholder concerns are legitimate business risks","There is almost certainly a middle-ground solution (abstracted progress, not exact GPS) but it has not been authorized","Courier location data has privacy/compliance implications that neither team has addressed"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which stakeholder priority should win when forced to choose","Whether legal constraints would independently resolve this by prohibiting exact GPS sharing","What level of location abstraction would satisfy Customer Success while protecting courier privacy","Whether drivers would accept any form of location sharing, even approximate"],"assumptions":["Both teams are acting in good faith based on real data","No executive has yet arbitrated this conflict","Legal/Compliance review has not been performed on courier location sharing","The business operates in jurisdictions where personal location data is regulated"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS visibility to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires zero location visibility to customers at all times during delivery. These requirements are mutually exclusive. Both teams have declared their requirement non-negotiable. This cannot be resolved at the engineering or product level — it requires executive arbitration on marketplace strategy: do we optimize for the customer side or the courier side of the marketplace, or do we find an authorized middle ground?","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success","Operations","Couriers","Customers","Legal/Compliance","Executive Leadership"],"options":["A: Side with Customer Success — show exact GPS. Accept driver attrition risk.","B: Side with Operations — show no location data. Accept competitive differentiation loss.","C: Negotiate a middle ground — show abstracted progress (e.g., 'X stops away', 'Nearby', ETA countdown) instead of exact GPS. This is the industry-standard compromise (Uber, DoorDash, Amazon all use abstracted tracking, not raw GPS).","D: Make it courier-opt-in — couriers choose whether to share location, creating inconsistent customer experience but respecting driver autonomy."],"recommendation":"Option C. The industry has already solved this problem. Exact GPS coordinates are neither necessary for customer transparency nor standard practice. Abstracted progress indicators ('Your delivery is 2 stops away', 'Courier is nearby', live ETA) provide the transparency Customer Success needs as a differentiator while eliminating the surveillance feeling Operations is reporting. However, this recommendation requires executive sign-off and Legal review before any engineering work begins. Do NOT start building until this conflict is resolved at the leadership level."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Legal/Compliance has not reviewed the proposal to share courier GPS coordinates with customers. In GDPR, CCPA, and many labor-law jurisdictions, courier real-time location is personal data. Sharing it with third parties (customers) may require explicit courier consent, a legitimate interest assessment, or may be prohibited entirely. This could independently invalidate the Customer Success requirement regardless of the business debate.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal/Compliance","Couriers","Executive Leadership"],"options":["Engage Legal/Compliance for a privacy impact assessment before any further product decisions","Proceed without legal review and accept regulatory risk"],"recommendation":"Mandatory Legal/Compliance review before any engineering work begins. This is not optional — it is a regulatory requirement in most jurisdictions."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Direct contradiction between two stakeholder requirements makes the spec unbuildable as stated","business_rule_broken":"Cannot simultaneously show and not show courier location to customers"},{"violation":"No Legal/Compliance review of personal location data sharing","business_rule_broken":"Courier GPS coordinates are personal data under GDPR/CCPA — sharing with customers without legal basis assessment is a compliance risk"},{"violation":"Spec treats this as a feature decision when it is a marketplace strategy decision","business_rule_broken":"Two-sided marketplace balance (customer experience vs. courier experience) must be arbitrated at executive level, not resolved by engineering"}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being scoped where customers would see their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator, while Operations demands zero location sharing due to driver retention problems. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that cannot be resolved at the engineering level
